description_long: This object is a large, coiled brass wind instrument with a wide, flared bell at one end and a long, smoothly curving tube that loops around itself. The bell is prominent and circular, opening outward in a dramatic flare, while the tubing forms a single, continuous spiral. The surface is matte and uniform in a muted brass or tan color, lacking any visible decorative elements or engravings. There are no visible valves, keys, or slides along the tubing, giving it a minimalist and streamlined appearance. The mouthpiece area is small and tapers off at the end of the coiled tube, with no detachable mouthpiece visible. The overall form is reminiscent of a natural horn or early brass instrument, designed for producing resonant, projecting sounds in open spaces.
description: A large, coiled brass wind instrument with a wide, flared bell and smooth, looping tubing in a matte tan finish. It lacks visible valves or a detachable mouthpiece, giving it a minimalist, streamlined look.
description_view_0: The side view highlights the wide, flared bell and the smooth, continuous coil of the tubing.
description_view_1: From the rear, the circular bell opening is prominent, and the spiral of the tubing is clearly visible.
description_view_2: This angle emphasizes the tight curvature of the tubing and the gradual flare of the bell.
description_view_3: A front-facing view into the bell, showing the depth and roundness of the opening, with the coiled tubing visible behind.
attribute_bell_flare: The bell is wide and features a gradual, dramatic flare, creating a large, open mouth.
attribute_valve_configuration: No valves are present; the tubing is continuous and unbroken.
attribute_leadpipe_curvature: The leadpipe is tightly curved, forming a smooth spiral that loops around itself.
attribute_tuning_slide_location: No visible tuning slide; the tubing appears continuous without distinct tuning slide features.
